tasks.criterion: Planner agent declares adaptive thinking frontmatter at effort medium: agents/planner.md's YAML frontmatter declares a thinking key as a single-line YAML mapping in the form thinking: { type: adaptive, effort: medium } (inline format).
tasks.verification_command: awk 'BEGIN{c=0} /^---$/{c++; if (c==2) exit; next} c==1' agents/planner.md | grep -qE 'thinking:.*type:.*adaptive.*effort:.*medium'

tasks.criterion: Generator agent declares adaptive thinking frontmatter at effort medium: agents/generator.md's YAML frontmatter declares a thinking key as a single-line YAML mapping in the form thinking: { type: adaptive, effort: medium } (inline format; default for IMPLEMENTATION mode with per-mode overrides described in body).
tasks.verification_command: awk 'BEGIN{c=0} /^---$/{c++; if (c==2) exit; next} c==1' agents/generator.md | grep -qE 'thinking:.*type:.*adaptive.*effort:.*medium'

tasks.criterion: Evaluator agent declares adaptive thinking frontmatter at effort high: agents/evaluator.md's YAML frontmatter declares a thinking key as a single-line YAML mapping in the form thinking: { type: adaptive, effort: high } (inline format; default for capability EVALUATION mode, with CONTRACT_REVIEW using max and regression-criterion evaluation using medium per the body policy section).
tasks.verification_command: awk 'BEGIN{c=0} /^---$/{c++; if (c==2) exit; next} c==1' agents/evaluator.md | grep -qE 'thinking:.*type:.*adaptive.*effort:.*high'

tasks.criterion: Evaluator body documents per-mode effort overrides and removes 'policy-only' disclaimer: agents/evaluator.md's 'Thinking Effort: Regression vs Capability Evaluation' section explicitly names medium, high, max and ties each to a mode (regression eval / capability eval / contract review). The Sprint-7 disclaimer text must be removed or rewritten to reflect that the frontmatter now exists.
tasks.verification_command: grep -qiE 'medium' agents/evaluator.md && grep -qiE 'high' agents/evaluator.md && grep -qiE 'max' agents/evaluator.md && ! grep -q 'policy-only section until Sprint 8' agents/evaluator.md && ! grep -q 'does not yet declare' agents/evaluator.md

tasks.criterion: harness-summary skill declares adaptive thinking at effort max: skills/harness-summary/SKILL.md's YAML frontmatter declares a thinking key as a single-line YAML mapping in the form thinking: { type: adaptive, effort: max } (inline format; analysis-heavy).
tasks.verification_command: awk 'BEGIN{c=0} /^---$/{c++; if (c==2) exit; next} c==1' skills/harness-summary/SKILL.md | grep -qE 'thinking:.*type:.*adaptive.*effort:.*max'

tasks.criterion: config.json declares thinking.profile field: .harness/config.json contains a top-level thinking object with a profile key whose value is one of 'default', 'fast', or 'thorough'. Default is 'default'.

config.json declares batch.enabled and batch.min_criteria fields: .harness/config.json contains a top-level batch object with keys enabled (boolean, default false) and min_criteria (integer, default 20).

harness-sprint SKILL contains a Batch API section in Step 3: skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md contains a Batch API section that explicitly references config.batch.enabled, config.batch.min_criteria, the trigger rule (enabled AND criterion count meets min_criteria), and the documented 50% discount and 24-hour SLA.
tasks.verification_command: grep -qiE 'batch.api' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md && grep -q 'batch.enabled' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md && grep -q 'batch.min_criteria' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md && grep -qiE '50%|fifty percent' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md && grep -qiE '24.hour|twenty.four' skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md

tasks.criterion: README documents new config knobs: README.md documents both the thinking.profile config knob (each of its three valid values appears verbatim somewhere in the file) and the batch.enabled / batch.min_criteria config knobs (with the discount/SLA tradeoff).
tasks.verification_command: grep -q 'thinking.profile' README.md && grep -q '"default"' README.md && grep -q '"fast"' README.md && grep -q '"thorough"' README.md && grep -q 'batch.enabled' README.md && grep -q 'batch.min_criteria' README.md

tasks.criterion: sprint-08.tasks.json is emitted: file exists, valid JSON, top-level sprint:8 and tasks array with 13 success criteria + 3 Should-NOT gates = 16 entries minimum, each with the Sprint-6 schema fields task_id, criterion, grader_type, weight, is_gate, verification_command, rubric_dimension.
tasks.verification_command: jq -e '.sprint == 8 and (.tasks | length) >= 16 and (.tasks | all(has("task_id") and has("grader_type") and has("weight") and has("is_gate")))' .harness/contracts/sprint-08.tasks.json

tasks.criterion: Backward compatibility is explicit and correct: README.md, skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md, and the agent files together make it unambiguous that a project whose .harness/config.json predates Phase 2 (no thinking object, no batch object) experiences no behavior change. A reader should be able to identify, for each new config field, the default value and the sentence that confirms it preserves Phase-1 behavior.

Per-role thinking effort rationale is justified, not just asserted: documentation explains why each role gets the effort level it does — planner/generator medium because routine; evaluator capability mode high because open-ended; contract review and summary analysis max because mistakes propagate; regression-criterion evaluation medium because pre-calibrated.

Batch API trigger condition, payoff, and result shape are well-defined: skills/harness-sprint/SKILL.md Batch API section states the precise trigger (config.batch.enabled == true AND criterion count >= config.batch.min_criteria), documents the payoff (50% discount, 24-hour SLA), and states that per-criterion result shape is preserved when batched.

No silent behavior change for pre-Phase-2 configs: A project whose .harness/config.json lacks the thinking object and the batch object must run the harness identically to Phase 1: agent effort defaults to whatever the runtime would do without frontmatter, no batch submission is attempted, and the evaluator runs every criterion synchronously as today.
No modification of prior sprint artifacts: .harness/evals/sprint-0[1-7]* and .harness/contracts/sprint-0[1-7]* must be unchanged from HEAD at the start of this sprint.

No invented Batch API semantics that contradict Anthropic's published contract: The Batch API section must not claim discount or SLA values that diverge from 50% / 24-hour, and must not assert that batched calls return results faster than synchronous calls.
